item.title: Oh no! Shari'a Law!
item.description: Zohran Mamdani has turned down an 18% pay rise that would have brought his salary to $305,800. The same day, members of the city council voted to approve a pay raise for themselves and other officials.

item.title: Carefully Curated Crap, Vol. 905
item.description:
1

23
24
25
26 Man Tax: Aleksander D'Avignon
"All the water in the world cannot sink a ship unless it gets inside the ship. All the negativity in the world cannot sink a person unless it gets inside the mind." - Mary Pickford So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc.
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Well, that was… unexpected
item.description: MV edit: hope everyone got some joy from this post. In retrospect, I probably should’ve kept the original creator in the crop, their handle is r/mattxiv. Thanks for the hilarious shit, friendo My recommendation to everyone else is to incorporate this line, randomly, into a conversation with zero context or pre-cursor. Possibly when there’s a brief lull in the conversation. Just throw it out there, but also don’t get in trouble with HR. Y’all stay safe _____ When I tell y’all I absolutely cackled at this shit
item.dc:creator: TheOlDickTwist

item.title: Basset Hounds
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.description: For nearly 16 years, Marlo Spaeth worked at a Walmart store in Manitowoc, Wisconsin. A woman with Down syndrome, she was known as a reliable employee and received positive performance reviews throughout her time at the store. Marlo depended on a regular daily routine because of her disability. She worked from 12 p.m. to 4 p.m., which allowed her to eat dinner at the same time every day. In 2014, Walmart introduced a new computerized scheduling system. Marlo's shift was changed to 1 p.m. to 5:30 p.m., disrupting the routine she relied on. Marlo and her sister, who was also her legal guardian, repeatedly asked the company to restore her original hours as a reasonable accommodation. They said the schedule change was causing serious difficulties for her. According to court records, Walmart refused the request. In July 2015, Marlo was fired because of attendance issues linked to the schedule change. She later tried to get her job back, but the company declined to rehire her. The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) filed a lawsuit on her behalf, arguing that Walmart had failed to provide a reasonable accommodation under the Americans with Disabilities Act. A federal jury ruled in Marlo's favor and awarded $125.15 million in damages. However, because of a federal legal limit on ADA damage awards for large employers, the amount she could actually receive was reduced to $300,000. Walmart appealed the decision, but in 2024, a federal appeals court upheld the jury's finding that the company had violated the law, bringing Marlo's long legal fight to a successful end....
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

I guess I'll never learn. Have as good a day as you can! Stay safe, be kind.

item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: Imgur is still the best
item.description: I know this site is a pale shadow of what it once was but it’s still full of wonderful and amazing people. I posted something earlier tonight and got a bunch of thoughtful and genuine replies to it that I doubt I would get on most social media. I still love this site and this community even tho it’s dying at the hands of private equity and capitalism Edit: lol this went to front page after 60 likes. Yep… this site sure is a skeleton.
item.dc:creator: RocketKokket

item.title: Amazing the Minton Tiles
item.description: It’s time…The wooden covering has been lifted, and the magnificent Minton Tiles have been revealed once again! Watch the transformation in this timelapse as one of Liverpool’s most spectacular hidden treasures is revealed beneath St George’s Hall. https://www.aoc.gov/explore-capitol-campus/art/minton-tiles
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: GovBrief Today #532
item.description: Donald Trump turned years of election lies into orders. He stood in the East Room Thursday night repeating claims his own declassified documents don't support, the same story he's told since 2016. The orders came after: he directed DHS to tell states to purge their voter rolls and told the FBI, CIA, and Justice Department to investigate, fire, and possibly charge the intelligence officials he says hid the evidence. The midterms are four months away. No president is allowed to give those orders. Elections belong to the states, and the fraud he described has lost in every courtroom that heard it. What changed is that he believes his power is unchecked. That's why he threatened the broadcast licenses of ABC and NBC when they wouldn't carry his speech live. I've emailed 8,265 stories over 532 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260716-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/smoke-pollution [2] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july16 [3] https://go.govbrief.today/cholesterol-pill [4] https://go.govbrief.today/taylor-farm-lettuce [5] https://go.govbrief.today/blanche-survivors [6] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-elections [7] https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-escorts [8] https://go.govbrief.today/abc-nbc-threat [9] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-ai-brief [10] https://go.govbrief.today/husky-award [11] https://go.govbrief.today/public-charge [12] https://go.govbrief.today/brouillette [13] https://go.govbrief.today/alaska-attorney-ice [14] https://go.govbrief.today/vegas-airport-ice [15] https://go.govbrief.today/truth-social-access [16] https://go.govbrief.today/teleprompter-bet [17] https://go.govbrief.today/nj-leave [18] https://go.govbrief.today/kies-ousted Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.description: I work as a carpenter, been off work for almost 2 years because of surgeries and physio. Just started work again 2 weeks ago, and I'm now helping the foreman directly and he's getting the big boss to get me a set of keys, as well as sending me the architectural drawings so I can run the setup
